channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: If you don’t trust Big Tech with your data, this is the chatbot for you
item.description: Sure, when chatbots aren’t outright hallucinating, they can be helpful tools for gathering information, generating ideas, and completing tasks. But some of the biggest players in the AI chatbot space—including OpenAI, Google, and Meta—aren’t exactly known for strong privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Twitter at 20: How we lost the public square
item.description: Twenty years ago, Jack Dorsey changed the world. He opened his phone and sent a message to a new platform he had created: “just setting up my twttr”. That post carries the ID 20.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The ‘toggle-away’ efficiencies: Cutting AI costs inside the training loop
item.description: “A single training run can emit as much CO₂ as five cars do in a year.” That finding from the University of Massachusetts, Amherst, has become the defining statistic of the generative AI era.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia joins push for data centers in space
item.description: “We’re going to space,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced at the company’s GTC conference on Monday. But then he qualified those remarks. “We’ve already been out in space,” he said. The company’s chips are already in satellites in orbit above Earth.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Uber expands women driver preference nationwide
item.description: Uber's Women Preferences feature lets female riders request female drivers nationwide for added safety and comfort during their rides, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 9 reasons Java is still great
item.description: In a world obsessed with disruption, Java threads the needle between stability and innovation. It’s the ultimate syncretic platform, synthesizing the best ideas from functional programming, concurrency, cloud computing, and AI under a reliable, battle-tested umbrella.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Genealogy boom exposes personal data scammers can exploit
item.description: Using genealogy websites can expose personal data that scammers use to target families, according to experts warning about privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: We mistook event handling for architecture
item.description: Events are essential inputs to modern front-end systems. But when we mistake reactions for architecture, complexity quietly multiplies. Over time, many front-end architectures have come to resemble chains of reactions rather than models of structure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Robot firefighters enter burning buildings first
item.description: New robotic firefighting vehicles use thermal cameras and water cannons to locate fires and hazards before firefighters step inside a building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Nvidia’s DGX Rubin NVL8 runs on Intel Xeon 6
item.description: Nvidia has selected Intel’s Xeon 6 processors as the host CPUs for its Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems. The DGX Rubin NVL8 is part of Nvidia’s next flagship AI system portfolio, designed to help companies accelerate agentic AI adoption.
item.dc:creator: Network World
